subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- **Baby Unicorn cards** — Placed in the Nursery. Brought into stables by card effects. Not part of the main deck.
- **Basic Unicorn cards** — Simple unicorns with no special abilities. Count toward your stable total.
- **Magical Unicorn cards** — Unicorns with special triggered or passive abilities.
- **Magic cards** — One-time use cards with immediate effects (played during your Action Phase).
- **Instant cards** — Cards played at any time in reaction to another card (primarily Neigh cards).
- **Upgrade cards** — Positive effects placed in your stable that grant ongoing bonuses.
- **Downgrade cards** — Negative effects placed in an opponent's stable that impose penalties.

#### Reference
- **Reference cards** — Quick-reference cards summarizing turn phases and card types.

#### Total Card Count
- **135 cards** in the base game.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **No board** — Unstable Unicorns has no game board. Each player has a play area (their stable) in front of them.
2. **Create the Nursery** — Separate all Baby Unicorn cards from the deck. Place them face up in the center of the table. This is the Nursery.
3. **Create the draw pile** — Shuffle all remaining cards together to form the draw pile. Place it face down in the center.
4. **Create the discard pile** — Leave space next to the draw pile for a face-up discard pile.
5. **Stable areas** — Each player has open table space in front of them for their stable (where unicorns and upgrade/downgrade cards are placed).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Hand
- **Deal 5 cards** — Each player is dealt 5 cards from the draw pile.

#### Starting Stable
- **Baby Unicorn** — Each player takes 1 Baby Unicorn from the Nursery and places it in their stable. This is your first unicorn.

#### Player Count Adjustments
- **2-5 players** — Win condition is 7 unicorns in your stable.
- **6-8 players** — Win condition is reduced to 6 unicorns in your stable.

#### No Other Setup Required
- No coins, tokens, or trackers needed. The game is entirely card-based.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Determine first player** — The person whose birthday is closest to the current date goes first.
2. **Turn direction** — Play proceeds clockwise.
3. **Starting state** — Each player has 5 cards in hand and 1 Baby Unicorn in their stable.
4. **Begin play** — First player starts with the full turn sequence (including draw phase).

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each turn consists of 4 phases in order:

#### 1. Beginning of Turn Phase
- Resolve any effects that trigger at the beginning of your turn (from cards in your stable or others').

#### 2. Draw Phase
- **Draw 1 card** from the draw pile and add it to your hand.

#### 3. Action Phase
- **Play 1 card** from your hand OR **draw 1 additional card** from the draw pile.
- **Card options:** Play a Unicorn card (basic or magical) into your stable, play a Magic card for its effect, play an Upgrade into your stable, or play a Downgrade into an opponent's stable.
- You may choose to draw instead of playing a card if you prefer.

#### 4. End of Turn Phase
- Resolve any effects that trigger at the end of your turn.
- **Hand limit check** — If you have more than 7 cards in hand, discard down to 7.
- Pass to the next player clockwise.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Playing Unicorn Cards
- **Basic Unicorns** — Place in your stable. No special effects. Count toward your unicorn total.
- **Magical Unicorns** — Place in your stable. Their effects activate as described on the card (enter effects, passive effects, or triggered effects).
- Unicorns stay in your stable until removed by a card effect.

#### Playing Magic Cards
- One-time use cards. Read and resolve the effect, then discard the Magic card.
- Effects may include: destroying unicorns, stealing unicorns, returning cards, searching the deck, etc.

#### Playing Upgrade Cards
- Place in your own stable. Provides ongoing beneficial effects as long as it remains.
- Examples: extra actions, protection from effects, drawing extra cards.

#### Playing Downgrade Cards
- Place in an opponent's stable. Imposes ongoing negative effects.
- Examples: preventing unicorn play, forcing discards, limiting abilities.

#### Instant Cards (Neigh)
- **Neigh** — Can be played at any time when another player plays a card. Negates that card entirely. The negated card goes to the discard pile.
- **Super Neigh** — Works like Neigh but cannot itself be Neighed.
- **Neigh chains** — A Neigh can be Neighed by another player, which can be Neighed again, etc. The last Neigh in the chain determines whether the original card resolves.
- Instants are the only cards played outside of your Action Phase.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Baby Unicorns
- Baby Unicorns cannot be played from hand (they are never in your hand under normal rules).
- Baby Unicorns are brought into a stable from the Nursery by card effects.
- If a Baby Unicorn is destroyed or returned, it goes back to the Nursery (not the discard pile).
- Baby Unicorns count toward your unicorn total.

#### Sacrifice vs. Destroy vs. Return
- **Sacrifice** — You send your own card to the discard pile (usually as a cost).
- **Destroy** — A card effect removes a card from any stable to the discard pile.
- **Return to hand** — A card goes back to the owner's hand (Baby Unicorns return to the Nursery instead).

#### Card Effect Timing
- **When this card enters your stable** — Triggers once immediately upon placement.
- **If this card is in your stable at the beginning of your turn** — Triggers at the start of each of your turns.
- **If this card is in your stable** — Passive ongoing effect.

#### Protection and Immunity
- Some cards grant protection from certain effects (e.g., your unicorns cannot be destroyed).
- Protection only applies while the protective card is in your stable.

#### Empty Draw Pile
- If the draw pile is empty, shuffle the discard pile to form a new draw pile.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
- **2-5 players** — First player to have 7 or more Unicorn cards in their stable at the start of their turn wins.
- **6-8 players** — First player to have 6 or more Unicorn cards in their stable at the start of their turn wins.

#### Important Win Timing
- The win condition is checked at the beginning of a player's turn (during the Beginning of Turn Phase).
- If you reach 7 (or 6) unicorns during another player's turn, you do not win yet. Opponents have until your next turn to reduce your stable.

#### Scoring Model
- **No VP system** — Unstable Unicorns is a race to a unicorn count.
- **No final tally** — The game ends immediately when a player meets the win condition at the start of their turn.
- **Counting unicorns** — Only Unicorn cards count (Basic and Magical). Upgrade and Downgrade cards in your stable do NOT count toward the total.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Build quietly** — Avoid drawing attention by racing to 4-5 unicorns too fast. Players will gang up on the leader with Neigh cards and destruction effects.
- **Stockpile Neigh cards** — Having 1-2 Neigh cards in hand gives you defensive power. Don't play them frivolously.
- **Play Upgrades early** — Upgrade cards that give extra actions or draw power compound over time. Get them established before opponents have removal.
- **Downgrade aggressively** — If you draw a strong Downgrade, play it on a threatening opponent early to slow their growth.
- **Draw when unsure** — If your hand lacks good plays, take the extra draw instead of wasting a mediocre card.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Using Neigh too early** — Neighing a basic unicorn on turn 2 wastes your most powerful defensive card. Save Neigh for game-swinging plays.
- **Ignoring the leader** — If one player reaches 5-6 unicorns and no one acts, they will win on their next turn. Coordinate destruction.
- **Forgetting hand limit** — Hoarding cards past 7 means discarding valuable options. Plan your hand size.
- **Miscounting unicorns** — Upgrade and Downgrade cards in your stable do NOT count as unicorns. Only count actual Unicorn cards.
- **Overcommitting to one strategy** — Don't rely entirely on Magical Unicorn combos. If key pieces get Neighed or destroyed, you need a backup plan.
- **Neglecting Baby Unicorn effects** — Cards that bring Baby Unicorns from the Nursery add to your count without costing a card from hand.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **When to Neigh** — Save Neigh for cards that would win the game or devastate your position. Don't Neigh low-impact plays.
- **Target selection for destruction** — Remove the unicorn with the strongest passive effect, not just any unicorn. Magical Unicorns with ongoing abilities are high-priority targets.
- **Play card vs. draw** — If your hand has 2 or fewer cards, drawing to rebuild may be better than playing a mediocre unicorn.
- **Timing your push** — The ideal moment to surge from 4 to 7 unicorns is when opponents are out of Neigh cards or distracted by each other.
- **King-making awareness** — Destroying one player's unicorns while ignoring another can hand the game to the wrong person. Spread disruption across threats.
